economic_notes: SXDLP owns interests in SALP (aquaculture), KBPLP (Kilgard Business Park), WSLP (Wochmel Security), and SMSLP (management services). First Nation receives 56% of revenue from ISC and Province of BC. Settlement Trust holds proceeds from Upper Sumas IR6 Right-of-Way settlement. Gaming revenue present. Trans Mountain Pipeline funding received.
notes: ISC funding split between direct ISC ($4,183,664) and ISC contracts for service ($7,365,188); total ISC = $11,548,852. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($11,548,852) + Province of BC ($971,756) + First Nations Health Authority ($885,328) + Department of Natural Resources ($679,663) + CMHC and housing charges ($456,914) + Sto:lo Service Agency Society ($303,264) + First Nations Education Steering Committee ($154,816) + New Relationship Trust ($135787) + City of Abbotsford ($117,317) + First Nation Land Management Resource Center ($1,692) + Ottawa Trust income ($105) = $15,255,694 — however gains, gaming, lease and commercial revenues are OSR. Four GBEs accounted for by modified equity method (Sumas Clay Products Ltd., SFN Renewable Energy Ltd., Sema:th Xot:sa Developments Ltd., 1099212 B.C. Ltd.). Five partnerships accounted for by modified equity method (SXDLP, SALP, KBPLP, SMSLP, WSLP). Settlement Trust and Minors' Trust consolidated. Large deferred revenue balance of $13.2M primarily from ISC ($8.9M) and Province of BC ($2.9M). Expenses capitalized of -$1,349,370 net against expenses. Blueberry crop claim contingent liability. Loan guarantees to members up to $4M and to LPs/Corps totaling ~$9M.
